summary: Split the tournament-related tail of src/melee/gm/gm_1601.c into a new game-mode source/header pair, src/melee/gm/gmtou.c and src/melee/gm/gmtou.h, with matching split ranges and symbol metadata updates. The moved range begins at 0x8019B8C4 and ends before gm_19EF at 0x8019EFC4. The only implemented stub shown, fn_8019BA04, was not broken; it was moved into the new file, which explains the bot's apparent match regression for the old object and gain in the new object.
what_changed: Reduced the gm_1601 split range so src/melee/gm/gm_1601.c now ends at text address 0x8019B8C4 instead of 0x8019EFC4, with corresponding rodata, data, bss, sdata, sbss, and sdata2 end-address adjustments.

Added a new split entry for melee/gm/gmtou.c covering text 0x8019B8C4..0x8019EFC4 and the corresponding data ranges: rodata 0x803B7D18..0x803B7D50, data 0x803DA2E0..0x803DA3E0, bss 0x80479A58..0x80479A98, sdata 0x804D41B8..0x804D41F0, sbss 0x804D6660..0x804D6698, and sdata2 0x804DA878..0x804DA950.
Moved placeholder declarations/comments for functions from gm_8019B8C4 through fn_8019EF08 out of gm_1601.c and into the new gmtou.c file.
Moved the corresponding prototypes from gm_1601.h into a new gmtou.h header.
Kept fn_8019BA04 as an empty matching stub in the new gmtou.c file, changing its prototype in the new header to the concrete signature void fn_8019BA04(void).

Added many data:string annotations in config/GALE01/symbols.txt for labels in the moved data/sdata region, including lbl_803DA3A4, lbl_803DA3BC, lbl_803DA3D0, and many lbl_804D3F70 through lbl_804D4178 entries.
Adjusted lbl_803DA2E0 size from 0xF0 to 0xC4 and split out new local string symbols at 0x803DA3A4 and 0x803DA3BC.

Updated configure.py to include a new nonmatching game-mode object in the build list. The diff excerpt shows Object(NonMatching, "melee/gm/gmtour.c"), while the changed file and split entry are named gmtou.c; the evidence slice does not clarify whether this was a typo in the excerpt or a naming mismatch that was resolved elsewhere.
smart_moves: Chose the split boundary exactly at 0x8019B8C4, matching the first moved function placeholder gm_8019B8C4 and preserving the next object boundary at 0x8019EFC4.
Updated all relevant split sections, not just .text, which prevents data/rodata/sdata attribution drift after moving the source object.
Moved prototypes into a dedicated header rather than leaving tournament-mode symbols in gm_1601.h, keeping header ownership aligned with the new source file.
Documented the filename as a guess based on strings and tournament-game-code evidence, avoiding overclaiming the module name.
Annotated many moved small-data entries as strings, which helps the splitter and future decompilation work treat the data region more accurately.
Explicitly explained that fn_8019BA04's bot-reported breakage was caused by relocation to the new object, not an actual loss of match.
decomp_lessons: When splitting a large source file, update config/GALE01/splits.txt for every section affected by the boundary: .text, .rodata, .data, .bss, .sdata, .sbss, and .sdata2.
Move placeholder function comments and prototypes together so future decomp work can search the correct file/header for an address range.
A match dashboard may report a function as regressing in the old object when it has simply moved to a new object; verify object relocation before treating it as a real mismatch.
Use strings and data-region clues to propose tentative file names, but mark guessed filenames when the original source name is not proven.
After a file split, revisit symbols.txt metadata around the boundary because object sizes and string-vs-raw-data classification often need to be refined.
Keep the build object list in configure.py synchronized with split filenames; any discrepancy between gmtou.c and gmtour.c should be checked carefully in similar future splits.
naming_conventions: New include guard used the path-derived form MELEE_GM_GMTOU_H.
Unknown functions retained address-based names such as gm_8019B8C4 and fn_8019BA08 rather than inventing semantic names.
The new source file name gmtou.c appears to be a guessed abbreviation for tournament game code, based on strings in the file according to the added comment.
The split kept the gm_ prefix for some game-mode routines and fn_ prefix for less-classified routines, consistent with existing placeholder naming.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Performed a pure relocation/file split of nonmatching placeholders plus one empty matching stub, minimizing code changes while improving object organization.
Preserved fn_8019BA04 as void fn_8019BA04(void) {} in the new file, maintaining its 100% fuzzy match in the new object.
Used address-aligned split boundaries to separate gm_1601.c from the new tournament-mode object without overlapping gm_19EF.c.
Updated symbol data annotations to mark many labels as data:string, likely improving section reconstruction and future matching context.
Interpreted the match report correctly: gm_1601 fuzzy/matched percentages rose slightly, gmtou gained initial coverage, and fn_8019BA04 moved from the old object to the new object.
review_feedback: No formal reviews or review comments were present in the slice.
Issue comment noted that the bot thought fn_8019BA04 was broken, but the function had simply moved to the new gmtou.c file.

The reported match summary showed gm_1601 fuzzy_match 0.78% -> 0.82%, gm_1601 matched_code 0.71% -> 0.75%, gmtou fuzzy_match/matched_code 0.00% -> 0.03%, and fn_8019BA04 moving from 100% in the old location to 100% in the new location.
follow_up_queries: Check whether configure.py should reference melee/gm/gmtou.c or melee/gm/gmtour.c; the PR slice shows a possible mismatch between the new file name and the build object entry.
Search later PRs for gmtou.c or gmtour.c to see whether the guessed tournament filename was renamed or confirmed.
Review future decomp PRs for functions in the 0x8019B8C4..0x8019EFC4 range to reuse the dedicated gmtou.h ownership.
Inspect strings around lbl_803DA3A4, lbl_803DA3BC, and the lbl_804D3F70..lbl_804D4178 sdata range for semantic naming opportunities.
